mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects deteriorating manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production signals fail to predict negative 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent impose tight financial conditions that suppress equity valuations and corporate investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60 percent of high-rate instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly boost corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with consumption growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 baseline index signals severe supply-side contraction that triggers recession fears and equity sell-offs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of sub-100 INDPRO instances, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample periods.
